title: RefRecord v2
description: One detected cross-project bead reference, intent-based. Emitted per (source, target, location) tuple when the prose contains a sigil that establishes referential intent. The active sigil mode is surfaced in the envelope's `sigil_mode` field (strict|verb|permissive). v2 surfaces cross-project refs only (v1's load-bearing constraint; relaxing it regresses FP to ~85%).

target.description: Referenced bead ID. For unresolved external: deps this is the raw external:<project>:<suffix> string so agents can see the exact shape that failed to resolve.

location.description: Where inside the source bead the reference was found.

flags.description: Validation flags in fixed output order: broken, stale, orphaned_child, cross_project. Every v2 record carries cross_project because the cross-project-only filter is load-bearing.

sigil_kind.description: Syntactic evidence that established reference intent. `markdown_link` / `inline_code` / `ref_keyword` are strict-mode sigils. `verb` is added in verb mode. `bare_mention` is permissive-mode only. `external_dep` / `bare_dep` apply to refs found in dep fields, not prose.

truncated.description: Set to true when the source body exceeded the 1MB per-body cap and was truncated before sigil detection. Omitted (treated as false) otherwise.
